motivation: Reviewer 2 and the author both wanted to know whether the Layer-0 K pathology is unique to Qwen2-7B (n=1) or a recurring pattern. Screened 4 additional candidate models from 2023-2024 timeframe, focusing on Chinese lab models (similar era to Qwen2) and older models (EleutherAI GPT-J).

note_on_failed_candidates.Qwen-7B (2023 initial): Load failed -- uses old transformers remote code with DisjunctiveConstraint import that was removed in transformers >= 5. Workaround possible but skipped.
note_on_failed_candidates.Baichuan2-7B-Base: Multiple dependency/format issues (bitsandbytes, safetensors format, meta tensor). Skipped after 3 failed attempts.

qwen1_5_7b.note: Predecessor of Qwen2-7B. CLEAN profile means the pathology is NOT a Qwen-family trait. Architectural note: Qwen1.5-7B uses full MHA (32:32), whereas Qwen2-7B transitions to GQA (28:4). The pathology appeared in the MHA-to-GQA transition and was fixed by Qwen2.5.

yi_6b.note: Same GQA ratio (8:1) as Qwen2-7B but CLEAN. This refutes the hypothesis that the pathology is caused by GQA ratio itself.

gpt_j_6b.note: CRITICAL DATA POINT: K_max 18.15x (higher than Qwen2-7B's 17.23x) but AT LAYER 1, NOT Layer 0. Layer 0 K is only 9.31x (elevated but not extreme). WikiText-2 PPL verification shows naive4 ΔPPL = +0.03 (CLEAN). This is the natural control: high K magnitude at Layer 1 does NOT cause catastrophic failure. Confirms Layer 0 specifically matters.

summary: Hunt found no new catastrophic cases (n=1 unchanged), but discovered GPT-J-6B as an important natural control: K_max 18.15x at Layer 1 with Layer 0 K only 9.31x shows that high K magnitude alone is not sufficient for catastrophic failure. The Layer 0 position is critical. This finding motivated the subsequent causality experiment.
